entries.details: Integrated Verification pass for AC-058 (regression vs AC-001..AC-050 on open-source layout). All core checks pass: npm install && npm run build -> dist/index.js; npx tsc --noEmit -> clean; docker build --platform linux/amd64 -> image runs as UID 10001; relay-config.docker.yaml parses via loadConfig() without code changes; no vendor SDKs in package.json or source tree; .env.example has zero vendor env vars.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-019 (health_check). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 health_check request through stub to relay. Response: [{resourceId:'order-pg', type:'postgres', healthy:true, latencyMs:6}, {resourceId:'order-mongo', type:'mongodb', healthy:true, latencyMs:0}]. All 5 test groups passed: correct JSON-RPC 2.0 response shape, both drivers present, all latencyMs >=0, error-handling path verified (try/catch in HealthReporter.checkAll() catches throws, logs warn with {err,resourceId}, returns healthy:false+latencyMs, other drivers unaffected), dispatch routes health_check to healthReporter.checkAll(). No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-017 (list_resources). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 list_resources request (no params) to relay through the stub. Response: { jsonrpc:'2.0', id, result: [{ resourceId:'order-pg', type:'postgres', name:'Order Service PostgreSQL', database:'relay', readOnly:true }, { resourceId:'order-mongo', type:'mongodb', name:'Order Service MongoDB', database:'relay', readOnly:true }] }. All 17 shape/contract checks passed: jsonrpc=2.0, id echoed, no error key, result is array of 2, every entry has exactly {resourceId,type,name,database,readOnly:true}, readOnly===true for all, types postgres|mongodb, both expected resources present, response shape matches createResponse(id,result). No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-018 (describe_resource). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). All 3 test cases pass: (1) unknown resourceId 'nonexistent' returns error code -32602 with message 'Unknown resource: nonexistent', (2) valid order-pg returns {tables:[{table_name,table_type}], type:'postgres', database:'relay'}, (3) valid order-mongo returns {tables:[], type:'mongodb', database:'relay'}. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-020 (protocol: unknown method + handler error). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 requests through stub to relay. (1) Unknown methods unknown_method, invalidMethod, foobar, delete all return error code -32601 with message Unknown method: <method>. (2) execute on non-existent table triggers catch block: code -32603, driver error message propagated, error logged with {err, requestId} in relay logs. (3) health_check still works after error (both drivers healthy). Source code confirms non-Error throws produce Internal error fallback. 10/10 checks passed. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-028 (postgres-sql-parser). validateQuery tested at compiled dist boundary. All three acceptance-check conditions verified: (1) validateQuery('SELECT 1') returns {valid:true}; (2) validateQuery('INSERT INTO t VALUES (1)') returns {valid:false, reason:'Only SELECT statements are allowed, got INSERT'} (parser-succeeds path) and the textual fallback 'INSERT statements are not allowed' works when the parser throws on malformed SQL; (3) validateQuery('SELECT 1; DROP TABLE users') returns {valid:false, reason:'Multi-statement queries are not allowed'}. Project builds cleanly (npm run build -> dist/). tsc --noEmit passes. PgDriver.validate() wires validateQuery for query/explain operations. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-022 (postgres-driver list_tables). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute request with operation:'list_tables' against resource 'order-pg' through the stub to the relay. Response rows: [{table_name:'orders', table_type:'BASE TABLE'}], rowCount: 1, fields: [{name:'table_name', type:'text'}, {name:'table_type', type:'text'}]. The query SELECT table_name, table_type FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'public' ORDER BY table_name runs against the real relay-postgres container and returns exactly the expected shape. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-023 (postgres-driver describe_table). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute request with operation:'describe_table', params:{tableName:'orders'} against resource 'order-pg' through the stub to the relay. Response rows: 5 column rows (id, status, customer, total_cents, created_at from information_schema.columns) + 1 constraint row (orders_pkey PRIMARY KEY on id tagged _type:'constraint' from information_schema.table_constraints). rowCount=6 matches rows.length. Column rows have column_name, data_type, is_nullable, column_default, character_maximum_length. Constraint rows have constraint_name, constraint_type, column_name, _type:'constraint'. The query runs information_schema.columns + information_schema.table_constraints scoped to public schema and orders table. 52/52 assertions passed. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-029 (postgres-sql-parser dangerous functions). validateQuery tested at compiled dist boundary. All 19 test cases pass: (1) validateQuery('SELECT pg_sleep(10)') => {valid:false, reason:'Dangerous function detected: pg_sleep'}; (2) validateQuery("SELECT * FROM pg_proc WHERE proname = 'pg_read_file'") => {valid:false, reason:'Dangerous function detected: pg_read_file'}; (3) all 15 documented dangerous functions (pg_sleep, lo_import, lo_export, pg_read_file, pg_write_file, pg_read_binary_file, pg_ls_dir, pg_stat_file, pg_terminate_backend, pg_cancel_backend, pg_reload_conf, set_config, current_setting, dblink, dblink_exec) are correctly rejected when their name appears anywhere in the lowercased query text, with the exact function name in the reason; (4) uppercase PG_SLEEP is also caught via lowercased text check; (5) dblink_exec has priority over dblink for correct function name; (6) safe query 'SELECT 1' still returns {valid:true}. Project builds cleanly (npm run build -> dist/). tsc --noEmit passes. PgDriver.validate() correctly wires validateQuery for query/explain operations. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-030 (postgres-sql-parser). Tested at compiled dist boundary. All three acceptance-check conditions verified: (1) validateQuery("WITH x AS (SELECT 1) SELECT * FROM x") returns {valid:true} — parser.astify succeeds, stmt.type==="select", no dangerous functions found; (2) validateQuery("SELECT * FROM (SELECT pg_sleep(1)) s") returns {valid:false, reason:"Dangerous function detected: pg_sleep"} — parser.astify succeeds, text scan catches pg_sleep; (3) validateQuery("EXPLAIN SELECT 1") returns {valid:true} — parser throws on EXPLAIN, catch block fallback accepts via first-word EXPLAIN check. PgDriver.validate() delegates to validateQuery() for query/explain operations. npx tsc --noEmit passes. npm run build passes. No defect found.

QA pass for AC-024 (postgres-driver query). E2E test at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + relay-postgres + relay-mongo). 53/53 assertions pass: SELECT specific columns from orders returns 5 rows with correct fields (id:int4, status:text, customer:text, total_cents:int4, created_at:timestamptz), rowCount=5, executionTimeMs>0, fields each have name+type from pg dataTypeID via OID_MAP. SELECT with explicit LIMIT 1 returns 1 row. Bad SQL error returns -32603 with driver error propagated. SELECT 1 AS one returns rows=[{one:1}], field one:int4. Code inspection confirms: transaction wrapped in BEGIN READ ONLY + SET statement_timeout=30s + LIMIT + COMMIT; dedicated client from pool released in finally; error triggers ROLLBACK best-effort before throw. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-024 (postgres-driver query). Tested at real Postgres external boundary (relay-postgres:5432) via the compiled PgDriver class. All 5 PgDriver E2E tests pass: (1) SELECT 1 AS one, hello AS greeting returns rows, fields [{name:one,type:int4},{name:greeting,type:text}]; (2) orders table returns 5 rows with correct fields; (3) LIMIT clamping: maxRows=3 restricts to 3 rows; (4) bad SQL throws error with ROLLBACK; (5) INSERT rejected by READ ONLY. Code inspection confirms: BEGIN READ ONLY, SET statement_timeout=30s, LIMIT, COMMIT, dedicated client released in finally, best-effort ROLLBACK on error. No defects.

Integrated Verification re-pass for AC-025 (postgres-driver limit clamping). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). 25/25 black-box assertions pass: (1) no explicit limit defaults to maxRows=1000 returns all 5 rows; (2) limit=3 clamped to 3 rows ids [1,2,3]; (3) limit=5 returns all 5 rows ids [1,2,3,4,5]; (4) limit=2000 exceeds maxRowsPerQuery=1000 rejected by policy engine with -32600 error containing message; (5) limit=0 clamped to 0 rows; (6) limit=1000 (N === maxRows) returns all 5 rows. Code inspection confirms: PgDriver.execute uses Math.min((limit ?? maxRows), maxRows) and appends LIMIT <clamped>; PolicyEngine.evaluate rejects when limit > maxRowsPerQuery. npx tsc --noEmit passes. npm run build passes. No code changes to relay sources.

QA pass for AC-025 (postgres-driver limit clamping). E2E test at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + relay-postgres + relay-mongo). 23/23 assertions pass: (1) Policy engine rejects limit=5000 (>1000) with -32600 error 'Policy denied: Row limit 5000 exceeds maximum 1000'; (2) Policy engine rejects boundary limit=1001 (>1000) with -32600; (3) limit=3 returns exactly 3 rows (ids [1,2,3]) proving LIMIT 3 appended to SQL and min(3,1000)=3 clamp; (4) limit=0 returns 0 rows (min(0,1000)=0); (5) no explicit limit returns all 5 rows (maxRows=1000 default). Code inspection confirms PgDriver.execute uses Math.min((limit ?? maxRows), maxRows) for the defensive clamp and appends LIMIT <clamped>. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-025 (postgres-driver limit clamping). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). 25/25 assertions pass via scripts/qa/ac025-test.mjs: (1) no explicit limit defaults to maxRows=1000, returns all 5 rows; (2) limit=3 clamped to 3 rows ids [1,2,3]; (3) limit=5 returns all 5 rows ids [1,2,3,4,5]; (4) limit=2000 exceeds maxRowsPerQuery=1000 rejected by policy engine with code -32600 and message "Policy denied: Row limit 2000 exceeds maximum 1000"; (5) limit=0 returns 0 rows; (6) limit=1000 (N===maxRows) returns all 5 rows. AC-038 cross-validated same policy clamp for both Postgres and Mongo (5/5 tests pass). Code inspection confirms: PolicyEngine.evaluate rejects when N > maxRowsPerQuery; PgDriver.execute uses Math.min((limit ?? maxRows), maxRows) defensive clamp and appends LIMIT <clamped>. npx tsc --noEmit passes. npm run build passes. No defects.

Independent QA verification for AC-027 (PgDriver.healthCheck + close). Tested at real Postgres external boundary (relay-postgres:5432). All verifications pass: (1) healthCheck on working connection returns true, runs SELECT 1 (source-confirmed), uses try/catch with no re-throw; (2) healthCheck on unreachable host returns false without throwing; (3) close resolves without error on working and failed drivers, awaits pool.end(); (4) graceful-shutdown path in index.ts registers SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers, iterates drivers calling driver.close() with .catch(()={}) error swallowing, wsClient.close() precedes driver.close(); (5) IReadOnlyDriver port declares healthCheck(): Promise<boolean> and close(): Promise<void>; (6) E2E health_check RPC via WebSocket confirms order-pg reports healthy:true with latencyMs>=0; (7) tsc --noEmit clean; (8) npm run build clean. 31/31 independent assertions pass across 4 test suites. No defects found.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-021 (protocol: execute response shape with masking). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute request to relay through the control-plane stub. AC-021 specifies resourceId='main-pg' but deployed config uses 'order-pg'. Both tested: (1) main-pg returns -32600 'Policy denied: Unknown resource: main-pg' -- correct policy engine behavior for unknown resource (noted: AC parameterization mismatch vs config), (2) order-pg returns correct shape: {rows:Array(5), rowCount:5, fields:[{name:'id',type:'int4'},{name:'status',type:'text'}], executionTimeMs:2, masked:false, maskedFieldCount:0}. All 9 shape checks pass: rows is array, rowCount number, fields array with name+type, executionTimeMs number, masked boolean equals (maskedFieldCount > 0). PII masking verified: query with email+CPF data returns masked='***@***.***' and '***.***.***-**', masked=true, maskedFieldCount=2, non-PII text unchanged. Audit logs confirm success entry for order-pg (info, rowCount=5, maskedFieldCount=0, executionTimeMs=6) and denied entry for main-pg (warn, reason='Unknown resource: main-pg'). Minor note: AC-021 specifies 'main-pg' as resourceId but config has 'order-pg'; implementation correctly handles both paths.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-031 (mongo-driver list_tables). Tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + postgres + mongo). Seeded 3 test collections (ac031_verify_*, ac031_products, ac031_users) in relay-mongo. Connected to control-plane-stub, sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute request with operation:'list_tables', resourceId:'order-mongo' through the stub to the relay. Response: rows=[{name:'...',type:'collection'},{name:'...',type:'collection'},{name:'...',type:'collection'}], rowCount=3, executionTimeMs=6. All 7 shape checks pass: rows is array, every row has {name:string,type:string}, rowCount matches rows.length, executionTimeMs >=0, masked boolean present, maskedFieldCount present, masked consistent with maskedFieldCount>0. Driver source (mongo-driver.ts:49-52) confirms db.listCollections().toArray() and maps to {name,type}[]. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-027 (PgDriver.healthCheck + close). Tested at real Postgres external boundary (relay-postgres:5432). All 4 E2E tests pass on the actual PgDriver class: (1) healthCheck() returns true on reachable Postgres, runs SELECT 1 against pool; (2) close() awaits pool.end() and completes without throwing; (3) healthCheck() returns false (no re-throw) on unreachable host (192.0.2.1:5432, connectionTimeoutMillis=2000); (4) healthCheck() after close() returns false gracefully (catch block still works). Graceful-shutdown path in src/index.ts confirmed: SIGTERM/SIGINT handler calls wsClient.close() then iterates drivers with await driver.close().catch(() => {}). Source code confirms PgDriver.healthCheck uses try/catch with no re-throw, and close() awaits this.pool.end(). IReadOnlyDriver port declares both methods. npx tsc --noEmit clean. npm run build clean. No defects.

QA pass for AC-032 (mongo-driver describe_table). E2E test at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + relay-postgres + relay-mongo). Seeded orders collection with documents of varied types (string, number, object, array, null, Date) and 3 indexes. Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute request with operation:describe_table, params:{tableName:orders} against resource order-mongo through stub to relay. Response: 7 schema rows (_id:string, status:string, amount:number, customer:object, tags:object, notes:object/string, createdAt:object) + 4 index rows (_type:index with name and key). Type inference correctly records set of typeof values per field across 2 sample documents (notes correctly shows both object and string). Indexes() output merged with _type: index tag and name/key properties. rowCount=11 matches rows.length. executionTimeMs=32 valid. masked/maskedFieldCount present.

Independent QA verification for AC-032 (mongo-driver describe_table). Re-tested at real WebSocket boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub + relay-postgres + relay-mongo). Seeded orders collection with documents of varied types (string, number, object, array, null, Date) and 3 indexes (idx_status, idx_amount, compound customer_1_createdAt_-1). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute request with operation:describe_table, params:{tableName:orders} against resource order-mongo through stub to relay. Response matched spec: 7 schema rows with field+types (_id:string, status:string, amount:number, customer:object, tags:object, notes:[object,string], createdAt:object), 4 index rows tagged _type:index with name+key (including _id_, idx_status, idx_amount, customer_1_createdAt_-1). rowCount=11 matches rows.length, executionTimeMs=31, masked=false, maskedFieldCount=0. All 14 assertion groups passed.

Fresh E2E verification of AC-032 (mongo-driver describe_table). Ran black-box test via real WebSocket boundary (relay + control-plane-stub on port 5191 + relay-postgres + relay-mongo). Seeded orders collection with 2 varied-type documents + 3 indexes. Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute with operation:describe_table, params:{tableName:orders} against resource order-mongo. Response: 7 schema rows (correct typeof inference for string, number, object, array, null, Date fields; notes correctly shows [object,string]) + 4 index rows (_id_, idx_status, idx_amount, customer_1_createdAt_-1) tagged _type:index with name+key. rowCount=11, executionTimeMs=5, masked=false, maskedFieldCount=0. All 30+ assertions passed. Verify-first mode: AC passes at real external boundary. No code changes needed.

Re-verification pass for AC-032 (mongo-driver describe_table). Re-ran black-box test at real WebSocket boundary (relay + control-plane-stub on port 5191 + relay-postgres + relay-mongo). Orders collection reseeded with 2 documents (varied types: string, number, object, array, null, Date) + 3 indexes. Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute: operation=describe_table, params={tableName:orders}, resourceId=order-mongo. Response: 7 schema rows with correct typeof inference (_id:string, status:string, amount:number, customer:object, tags:object, notes:[object,string], createdAt:object) + 4 index rows tagged _type:index with name+key (_id_, idx_status, idx_amount, customer_1_createdAt_-1). rowCount=11, executionTimeMs=4, masked=false, maskedFieldCount=0. All 14 assertion groups passed. No code changes needed — implementation already conforms to AC-032 spec.

Independent QA verification pass for AC-032 (mongo-driver describe_table). Full black-box test at real WebSocket boundary through docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub on port 5191 + relay-mongo + relay-postgres). Orders collection reseeded with 2 documents covering varied types (string, number, object, array, null, Date) + 3 named indexes. Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute: operation=describe_table, params={tableName:orders}, resourceId=order-mongo. Response verified: jsonrpc=2.0, no error, rows array with 7 schema rows + 4 index rows = rowCount=11. Schema inference correct: _id=string, status=string, amount=number, customer=object, tags=object, notes=[object,string], createdAt=object. All index rows tagged _type=index with name+key (_id_, idx_status, idx_amount, customer_1_createdAt_-1). Compound index on customer+createdAt found. executionTimeMs=2ms, masked=false, maskedFieldCount=0. All 21 assertions passed. No defects found.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-033 (mongo-driver query with filter/sql/fallback). Tested at real WebSocket + MongoDB boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub on port 5191 + relay-postgres + relay-mongo). Seeded test_ac033 collection with 8 documents of varied status/score/tags. Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute request with operation:query against resource order-mongo through stub to relay. Verified: (A) filter={status:active} returns 4 rows; (B) sql='{"status":"inactive"}' returns 3 rows when filter is absent; (C) no filter and no sql returns all 8 rows (fallback to {}); (D) limit=3 returns 3 rows, limit=0 returns 0 rows (driver now special-cases MongoDB .limit(0) behavior), limit=2000 rejected by policy engine; (E) both filter and sql present, filter takes precedence. Result rows are Record<string, unknown>[]. All assertions passed.

Re-verification pass for AC-033 (mongo-driver query with filter/sql/fallback). Tested at real WebSocket + MongoDB boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub on port 5191 + relay-postgres + relay-mongo). Re-seeded test_ac033 collection with 8 documents. Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute requests with operation:query against resource order-mongo through stub to relay. All 5 scenarios verified: (A) filter={status:active} returns 4 rows; (B) sql='{status:inactive}' returns 3 rows when filter is absent; (C) no filter and no sql returns all 8 rows (fallback to {}); (D) limit=3 returns 3 rows, limit=0 returns 0 rows, limit=2000 rejected by policy engine; (E) both filter and sql present, filter takes precedence. Result rows are Record<string, unknown>[]. All assertions pass. Zero code changes needed, worktree clean.

Verified AC-034 (mongo-driver aggregation pipeline validation) at real WebSocket + MongoDB boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub on port 5191 + relay-postgres + relay-mongo). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute requests with operation:query and pipeline params against resource order-mongo. Test 1: pipeline with $out stage → rejected with error code -32602 and message "Validation failed: Aggregation stage $out is not allowed". Test 2: pipeline with $merge stage → rejected with error code -32602 and message "Validation failed: Aggregation stage $merge is not allowed". Test 3: pipeline with $match/$group → accepted with result.rows = [] (no matching data). Test 4: pipeline with $match/$sort/$project/$limit → accepted with result.rows = []. All 4 tests passed. Implementation is correct — no code changes needed.

Independent QA verification pass for AC-034 (mongo-driver aggregation pipeline validation). Tested at real WebSocket + MongoDB boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub on port 5191 + relay-postgres + relay-mongo). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute requests with operation:query and pipeline params against resource order-mongo through stub to relay. Test 1: pipeline with $out stage -> rejected with error code -32602 and message "Validation failed: Aggregation stage $out is not allowed". Test 2: pipeline with $merge stage -> rejected with error code -32602 and message "Validation failed: Aggregation stage $merge is not allowed". Test 3: pipeline with $match + $group -> accepted with result.rows = [], rowCount = 0. Test 4: pipeline with $match + $sort + $project + $limit -> accepted with result.rows = [], rowCount = 0. All 4 tests pass at the real WebSocket boundary. Source code confirms BLOCKED_AGGREGATION_STAGES = ["$out", "$merge"] in mongo-driver.ts. npx tsc --noEmit clean. npm run build clean. No defects.

Integrated Verification pass for AC-034 (mongo-driver aggregation pipeline validation). Tested at real WebSocket + MongoDB boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub on port 5191 + relay-postgres + relay-mongo). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute requests with operation:query and pipeline params against resource order-mongo through stub to relay. (1) Pipeline with $out -> rejected -32602 "Validation failed: Aggregation stage $out is not allowed". Integrated Verification pass for AC-035 (mongo-driver explain). Tested at real WebSocket + MongoDB boundary via running docker-compose stack (relay + control-plane-stub on port 5191 + relay-postgres + relay-mongo). Sent JSON-RPC 2.0 execute requests with operation:explain against resource order-mongo through stub to relay. Test 1: explain with filter={status:active} returns {rows:[<explanation with executionStats and queryPlanner>], rowCount:1, executionTimeMs:9}. Test 2: explain without filter returns same shape (rowCount:1). Test 3: explain on nonexistent collection succeeds gracefully (Mongo handles empty find explain). Response contains queryPlanner (namespace, parsedQuery, winningPlan, rejectedPlans) and executionStats (executionSuccess, nReturned, executionTimeMillis, totalDocsExamined, totalKeysExamined) as expected from collection.find(filter).explain('executionStats'). All 12 shape assertions passed. No code changes needed - implementation already conforms to AC-035 spec in mongo-driver.ts:90-100.
